title: User Settings Table for Feature Configuration
story: As a product administrator, I want to manage user-specific application settings through a configuration table, so that I can programmatically enable or disable features for individual users without code changes.
description:
Build a user settings/variables table that stores application configuration options per user (and eventually per organization). The table holds boolean feature flags and preference values with sensible defaults.

Initial settings include: `ShowMigration`, `ShowDictionary`, `ScorecardEditing`, and `ContentEditing`. New users receive all settings enabled by default. Users can update their settings via a dedicated settings page (`settings.php`).

The settings values are stored in the database and loaded into session variables on login. Feature visibility throughout the application (e.g., object page dropdowns, migration/dictionary toggles) is controlled by these session values.

A validation rule prevents users from disabling both ShowMigration and ShowDictionary simultaneously — unchecking one automatically re-enables the other to ensure at least one core feature remains active.
acceptance_criteria: A user settings table exists with columns for each configurable feature flag
New users are created with all settings enabled by default
Settings page at settings.php allows users to toggle feature flags
Settings are persisted to the database on save
Settings are loaded into session variables on login
Application pages respect the session-based feature flags for conditional rendering
Users cannot disable both ShowMigration and ShowDictionary simultaneously
Unchecking one of the mutually dependent settings automatically re-enables the other
Changing settings and returning to the objects page reflects the updated configuration immediately
ux_changes: New settings page (settings.php) with checkboxes for ShowMigration, ShowDictionary, ScorecardEditing, ContentEditing, and a database option dropdown. Mutual exclusion logic prevents both migration and dictionary from being disabled. Objects page dropdown options dynamically reflect the saved settings.
middleware_changes: On registration, default settings are written to the user settings table. On login, settings are loaded from the database into PHP session variables. Feature flag checks are applied throughout the application to conditionally render UI elements.
data_model_changes: New user settings table storing per-user feature flags: ShowMigration, ShowDictionary, ScorecardEditing, ContentEditing, plus a database preference field.
implementation_notes: Settings are stored as boolean (0/1) values in the database. Session variables are used as the runtime cache for feature flags. An edge case was found where changing from dictionary-only to migration-only did not immediately reflect on the objects page — this was caused by stale session data and was fixed.
comment_summary: Extended discussion about implementation approach. Developer confirmed new table creation, default values on registration, and session-based feature flag loading. An edge case with stale settings after toggling migration/dictionary was identified during QA and resolved. Validation logic was added to prevent both core features from being disabled simultaneously.
